title: array + length bounds
manager: Homogeneous arrays with optional length bounds — "a list of at most eight readings" — so the length becomes a type the checker can size, keeping the machine in the finite band. Bounded arrays are exactly what let a machine hold collections and still make finiteness claims; unbounded arrays are available too, for when you genuinely need them and are willing to leave the checkable band to get them.
cs: homogeneous array; optional length bound -> finite band; bracket/get + HOFs via the container protocol (W6.43).
